title: 16 CFR Part 255, Guides Concerning Use of Endorsements and Testimonials in Advertising
author: Federal Trade Commission
notes: Used for endorsement definitions, honest-opinion requirements, clear and conspicuous disclosure context, and advertiser guidance and monitoring responsibilities.

title: Disclosures 101 for Social Media Influencers
author: FTC staff
notes: Used for the narrow point that employment, financial, personal, or family relationships can be material connections that require clear disclosure when endorsing a brand.

title: FTC's Endorsement Guides: What People Are Asking
author: FTC staff
notes: Used for contextual guidance that FTC endorsement analysis is fact-specific and does not create a safe harbor.

title: LinkedIn Professional Community Policies
author: LinkedIn
notes: Used for LinkedIn's policy context on true identity, accurate information, false or misleading content, and clear notice for personal benefit.

title: Regulatory Notice 10-06, Social Media Web Sites
author: FINRA
notes: Used only for regulated financial-services context on social media communications, supervision, and recordkeeping.

title: Regulatory Notice 17-18, Guidance on Social Networking Websites and Business Communications
author: FINRA
notes: Used only for regulated-team routing context around digital communications, supervision, and content requirements.

title: Employee-Generated Content Infrastructure
author: Jeffery Schroeder
notes: Used for cluster continuity and internal linking, not external factual proof.

title: How To Scale Employee-Generated Content On LinkedIn
author: Jeffery Schroeder
notes: Used for cluster continuity and internal linking, not external factual proof.

title: How To Turn Employee Expertise Into Useful LinkedIn Posts
author: Jeffery Schroeder
notes: Used for cluster continuity and internal linking, not external factual proof.
